lesson.menu_title: What Do You Do? -- Your Professional World in Natural English
lesson.menu_desc: Describing your work with the present simple, and your current projects with the present continuous -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 3 -- What Do You Do?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcelo de Azevedo Souza | Aula 3 -- What Do You Do?

listenings.text: Hi Marcelo, it's Ingrid, from the cycling weekend. I really enjoyed hearing about what you do. It's funny, most people think logistics is just trucks and boxes, but the way you explained the cold chain for medicines really stayed with me. Listen, my brother runs a small food company here in Bergen, and right now he is looking for someone to talk to about how a supply chain actually works. I told him about you. No pressure at all, but if you have twenty minutes one evening, he would love to pick your brain over a video call. Let me know. And by the way, we are already planning next summer's ride. Take care.

Welcome back to the show. Today we are talking about the hidden world of logistics. Here is something most people don't realize: when you order medicine online, dozens of people and systems work together to get it to your door safely. A team plans the route, another team handles customs, and someone monitors the temperature every single hour. We call this the cold chain, and it never stops. Right now, as you listen to this, thousands of these deliveries are moving across the world. The challenge is simple to say and hard to do: the right product, in the right place, at the right temperature, at exactly the right time. It sounds simple, but actually it is one of the most complex jobs in the modern economy.
